page: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a base clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the middle tier for most standard compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1637.0 and a multi-core score of 15823.0. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, such as image processing with a Geekbench score of 15040.0 and cryptographic operations, scoring 43448.8 in the Passmark CPU encryption test. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 158.1 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is measured at 69.22 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6in.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require high network throughput without the premium cost of dedicated GPU hardware or massive memory arrays. The 75 Gbps network bandwidth makes it highly suitable for network-intensive applications, database operations, static web serving, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It is configured with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor offering 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a clock speed of 3.5 GHz, alongside 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. While it lacks local storage, it features a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across general compute tasks, with strong results in image processing and CPU encryption tests. The instance is well-suited for network-intensive workloads, enterprise database operations, static web serving, and moderate-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for network-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed Intel Xeon processors and high-bandwidth network connectivity for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
192.0 GB of DDR4 system memory with 4.0 GB per vCPU
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads
Strong performance in cryptographic and image processing benchmarks
Optimized for network-intensive databases, web serving, and inference
